title: Kaworus role in Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu's role in Instrumentality             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jun 30, 2010 5:52 am This time I'm going to split the discussion by myself considering that indeed this is little related to " Project E Stuff "; I've tried to search for old threads related to this argument but failed to find one. Killer Bee wrote: I agree that Yui was the mastermind behind the whole thing, but she obviously couldn't do much without Gendo's help in the "real world" while she's stuck inside Unit-01. Also, SEELE never intended to use Unit-01 to the extent that Gendo did. They wanted Kaworu to initiate Third Impact by contacting Lilith and thus start Instrumentality. Gendo and Yui, however, wanted to use Unit-01 and Shinji. To SEELE, Unit-01 was merely the Test-Type. To Gendo and Yui, Unit-01 was the key to Instrumentality.

[]: Re: Kaworu's role in Instrumentality             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Wed Jun 30, 2010 1:35 pm Well, after Kaworu screwed things up in 24, SEELE had no choice but to use Unit-01 and the MPEs to start Instrumentality. Kaworu had failed them and only one option was left: Hijack Gendo's(Yui's) plan to use Unit-01. It has been several years since I've actually watched NGE, so if someone could point out what I'm missing, that'd be great. If Kaworu wasn't SEELE's key to Instrumentality in the TV series, why did they bother getting him into NERV at all? What was his purpose if not to poke Lilith?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jun 30, 2010 1:54 pm They sent him on a suicide mission so that he could get killed, and hopefully take Unit 02 out of the picture with him. No brainer.

[]:             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Wed Jun 30, 2010 2:08 pm AuraTwilight wrote: They sent him on a suicide mission so that he could get killed, and hopefully take Unit 02 out of the picture with him. No brainer.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jun 30, 2010 3:39 pm Killer Bee wrote: The only reason Kaworu got killed was because he allowed it. He got disenchanted with SEELE and wanted Shinji to take his life rather than deal with the consequences SEELE had set up for him. That wasn't in SEELE's playbook. Why would SEELE create Kaworu only to throw him away? EDIT: I just read the OP again. So, what you're saying is, by the time Kaworu descended into Central Dogma, SEELE had already given up on him and decided to go with the Unit-01 scenario instead?

[]:             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Wed Jun 30, 2010 3:54 pm [code:1]Scene: SEELE monoliths SEELE ??: Tabrith... SEELE ??: All was well up to the opening of Heaven's Door, SEELE ??: But at that final moment did he betray us? SEELE 08: It would be wrong to blame him. If one were to think that this was the scenario from the start. SEELE 03: Indeed. SEELE 04: That he was the last Angel is true, but nowhere is it written in the Dead Sea Scrolls that he was the successor of life.[/code:1] Wow, that's very specific. It's like they're discussing my actual posts. Sounds like they never planned to use Kaworu to initiate 3I after all. SEELE 08's words make it sound like putting Kaworu to use was decided on little more than a whim. So... yeah. That pretty much kills my theory dead.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:10 pm Killer Bee wrote: Is... that sarcasm? The only reason Kaworu got killed was because he allowed it. He got disenchanted with SEELE and wanted Shinji to take his life rather than deal with the consequences SEELE had set up for him. That wasn't in SEELE's playbook. Why would SEELE create Kaworu only to throw him away? EDIT: I just read the OP again. So, what you're saying is, by the time Kaworu descended into Central Dogma, SEELE had already given up on him and decided to go with the Unit-01 scenario instead?

[]:             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:13 pm AuraTwilight wrote: No sarcasm here. I'm not at all convinced that SEELE deliberately created Kaworu or anything like that, and even if he was, it doesn't change the fact that his death is useful. Kaworu's mission was doomed to the start; once he found that it was Lilith in the basement, he realizes SEELE lied to him, and victory was never really possible from the start. That's why he let Shinji kill him most likely. I interpret the Tabris name as being ironic; Kaworu is the only Angel that not only has no choice in his form, but also had no real chance to get what he actually desired. His act of self-sacrifice wasn't a win VS lose scenario, it was a lose VS lose on my own terms scenario.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:51 pm I've always found the discussion with the Seele a little contradictory in this regard; they told him that the regenerated body of Adam was already in Gendo so wasn't it obvious that there couldn't be Adam inside Terminal Dogma? Or the regenerated corp of Adam was something different from the actual Adam in Kaworu's terms. This is actually a discussion that it's part of the NPC and that could be considered the source of the contradiction but I'm not too sure about it. Also he could have joined with Lilith as well, according to the CI actually some Angels (of course they don't bother telling us which of them and how they discovered it) knew that there was Lilith inside the Geofront yet they were trying to reach her to "reset all life" (what could have happened after that is a little obscure for me). That's why he let Shinji kill him most likely. I interpret the Tabris name as being ironic; Kaworu is the only Angel that not only has no choice in his form, but also had no real chance to get what he actually desired. His act of self-sacrifice wasn't a win VS lose scenario, it was a lose VS lose on my own terms scenario.

[]:             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Wed Jun 30, 2010 5:17 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: according to the CI actually some Angels (of course they don't bother telling us which of them and how they discovered it) knew that there was Lilith inside the Geofront yet they were trying to reach her to "reset all life" (what could have happened after that is a little obscure for me).

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jun 30, 2010 5:27 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I've always found the discussion with the Seele a little contradictory in this regard; they told him that the regenerated body of Adam was already in Gendo so wasn't it obvious that there couldn't be Adam inside Terminal Dogma? Or the regenerated corp of Adam was something different from the actual Adam in Kaworu's terms. This is actually a discussion that it's part of the NPC and that could be considered the source of the contradiction but I'm not too sure about it. Also he could have joined with Lilith as well, according to the CI actually some Angels (of course they don't bother telling us which of them and how they discovered it) knew that there was Lilith inside the Geofront yet they were trying to reach her to "reset all life" (what could have happened after that is a little obscure for me).

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jun 30, 2010 5:52 pm Monk Ed wrote: What I presented in the Ep 24' Plothole/Fanwank As for the Lilith thing, what I figured was that Kaworu alone among the Angels could not use Lilith, because he did not have the necessary parts. For example, he probably lacked an S2. (Being so small he wouldn't have needed one, and Rei is able to do all the same flying and ATF projecting that he does without one, plus he was an artificial incarnation of a soul rather than a natural-born Angel.) Likewise, Kaworu alone would have been able to do Very Bad Things with the original body of Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jun 30, 2010 6:10 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: But if that's true (and I doubt it) then what he mentioned to Shinji that only one life form can survive and that if he doesn't kill him they'll be the one to disappear wasn't a real or at least an immediate thread.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jun 30, 2010 6:12 pm I've always found the discussion with the Seele a little contradictory in this regard; they told him that the regenerated body of Adam was already in Gendo so wasn't it obvious that there couldn't be Adam inside Terminal Dogma? Or the regenerated corp of Adam was something different from the actual Adam in Kaworu's terms. This is actually a discussion that it's part of the NPC and that could be considered the source of the contradiction but I'm not too sure about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jun 30, 2010 7:23 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Besides, you might be misinterpreting the CI. I take that line to mean that the Angels are heading towards Lilith thinking it is Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Jun 30, 2010 10:10 pm I'll just drop this baby here and walk away whistling... I've pretty much exhausted myself on this topic and the Eva Wiki article is likely as definitive as can possibly be on the matter ATM.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jul 01, 2010 12:47 am And we're really starting to get Kaworu as an idiot that can't even think about he reason they want Unit 02 out of the picture (if that was part of Seele's plan to reduce the strength of Nerv's self-defence forces).

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jul 01, 2010 1:08 am AuraTwilight wrote: He only shows himself surviving in space in Rebuild, mind you. The rules could be different.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Jul 01, 2010 1:26 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: But you're still giving as obvious that Kaworu doesn't have one while he says that "I've been destined to live forever" and as I mentioned he can do things like surviving in space that only beings with the Fruit of Life could do.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jul 01, 2010 1:39 am SaltyJoe wrote: But if he did have an S^2 and all the stuff required to qualify as a full blown Adam: well, what would be his motivation to search for "his" body?
